description: Measures whether a person believes outcomes are controlled internally (by themselves) or externally (by luck, chance, or others). Based on Rotter's I-E Scale (1966).

scale.scoring_note: Each B answer scores 1 point. Total B count = external locus score.

internal.label: Internal Locus of Control
internal.description: Strong belief in personal control over life outcomes.

mixed.label: Mixed Locus of Control
mixed.description: Control beliefs vary depending on the situation.

external.label: External Locus of Control
external.description: Outcomes are largely attributed to luck, chance, or other people.

A.text: People's failures are mostly the result of their own mistakes.

B.text: Many failures happen due to bad luck beyond a person's control.

example_answer.explanation: I believe most outcomes follow from the choices a person makes.

A.text: In the long run, people get the respect they deserve.

B.text: A person's worth often goes unrecognized no matter how hard they try.

example_answer.explanation: I feel that recognition depends heavily on who is watching, not just effort.

A.text: When I make plans, I am almost certain I can make them work.

B.text: It is not always wise to plan too far ahead since many things depend on chance.

example_answer.explanation: I trust my ability to execute a plan once I commit to it.

A.text: Becoming a success is a matter of hard work — luck has little or nothing to do with it.

B.text: Getting a good opportunity depends mainly on being in the right place at the right time.

example_answer.explanation: I believe consistent effort is the main driver of success in my life.

A.text: The results I get are directly related to how much effort I put in.

B.text: Sometimes I cannot understand how evaluators or decision-makers arrive at their conclusions.

example_answer.explanation: I often feel results are unpredictable regardless of how prepared I am.

A.text: I am the master of my own fate.

B.text: A great deal of what happens to me is probably just a matter of chance.

example_answer.explanation: I strongly feel my decisions shape the direction of my life.

A.text: People are lonely because they do not try hard enough to be friendly.

B.text: There is not much use in trying too hard to please people — if they like you, they like you.

example_answer.explanation: I think social connections are built through consistent effort and openness.

A.text: What happens to me is my own doing.

B.text: Sometimes I feel I do not have enough control over the direction my life is taking.

example_answer.explanation: I sometimes feel like life is happening to me rather than because of me.

A.text: With enough effort, people can bring about meaningful change in society.

B.text: It is difficult for ordinary people to have much control over what powerful institutions do.

example_answer.explanation: I feel that large systems operate independently of individual influence.
